purpose: Gap analysis between the v3 React/JSX design prototype dashboard and the current Hono SSR portal implementation. Identifies every meaningful UI element delta, exact token/px/interaction differences, and the portal files to change.

gaps.design_target: design-tokens.css dark: --phase-prd:#d6c4ee, --phase-tdd:#3d6f94, --phase-plan:#a8e0c4, --phase-spec:#6f6c20, --phase-code:#f5b888, --phase-review:#955d35, --phase-deploy:#c4e8c6, --phase-observe:#706e5c. Alternating light/dark for >=3:1 peer-chip contrast.
gaps.current_state: portal static/app.css dark block (lines 46-53) uses its own palette: --phase-prd:#a48bd9, --phase-tdd:#6fa8c7, --phase-plan:#66b896, --phase-spec:#b5b250, --phase-code:#e89255 (same as --brand), --phase-review:#c98a55, --phase-deploy:#98c39a, --phase-observe:#9a978a. Portal static/design-tokens.css dark block matches the design exactly — but app.css *overrides* it, so the design values never win at runtime.
gaps.notes: Delete the phase-color overrides in static/app.css dark block (lines 46-53) so design-tokens.css values prevail. No JSX changes needed.

gaps.design_target: design's app.css defines three density tiers driven by [data-density] on <html>: comfortable (--row-y:12px, --pad-card:16px, --pad-section:28px), compact (6/12/16), default root (10/14/22). Density toggle not shown in dashboard.jsx but the token cascade drives all section/card padding.
gaps.current_state: No data-density attribute or density-tier CSS variables exist anywhere in the portal. Spacing is hard-coded in shell.css (.main padding: var(--s-8) var(--s-10) = 32px 40px) and app.css (.kpi-strip margin: 18px 0 24px, .sec margin-bottom: 28px). The design's --pad-section and --pad-card tokens are absent.
gaps.notes: Add the three density tiers to design-tokens.css (or static/app.css), wire --pad-card and --pad-section into .kpi, .card-b, .main-inner/.main padding, and .sec margin. ShellLayout needs data-density={userPreference} passed from request context. Could be deferred — the default tier visually matches well enough.

gaps.area: App shell — main content scroll model
gaps.design_target: design app.css: body { overflow: hidden } + .main { height:100vh; overflow-y:auto } — the main column is the scroll container, not the document. This is required for the sticky topbar (position:sticky inside .main) to work correctly.
gaps.current_state: portal shell.css: .main has padding and max-width but no height:100vh/overflow-y:auto. Document-level scroll is used. The sticky rail works (align-self:start + position:sticky on .rail) but a sticky topbar would not work with the current model.
gaps.notes: When the topbar is introduced (.topbar { position:sticky; top:0 } — see topbar gap below) the scroll model must flip to .main { height:100vh; overflow-y:auto } and body { overflow:hidden }. Impacts all existing sticky table headers (.tbl th { position:sticky }) — verify they still work inside the inner scroll container.

gaps.area: App shell — main inner padding / max-width
gaps.design_target: design app.css: .main-inner { padding: var(--pad-section) 32px; max-width:1480px; margin: 0 auto } — content lives in .main-inner, not .main itself. Allows .topbar to span full .main width while content stays capped.
gaps.current_state: portal shell.css: .main { padding: var(--s-8) var(--s-10); max-width:1280px } — padding and cap are on .main itself. No .main-inner wrapper. Max-width is 1280px vs design's 1480px.
gaps.notes: Requires adding a <div class='main-inner'> wrapper inside <main class='main'> in ShellLayout, moving padding from .main to .main-inner, and raising max-width to 1480px. All existing views will gain extra breathing room at wide viewports.

gaps.design_target: design app.css .topbar: position:sticky; top:0; z-index:10; background: color-mix(in oklab, var(--bg-0) 90%, transparent); backdrop-filter:blur(8px); border-bottom:1px solid var(--line-1). Inner: 12px 32px padding, max-width:1480px, flex row. Contains: h1 (17px/600, -0.005em), .sub (12px mono fg-2), spacer, live-indicator dot+STREAMING, .meta-mono.dim 'updated 0.4s ago', Refresh button, 'Pause feed' ghost button.
gaps.current_state: No .topbar component exists. The page head is rendered as <div class='page-head'> inside #dashboard-body (dashboard.tsx line 192) — a plain flex row with h1+head-actions, no sticky positioning, no frosted glass, no live streaming indicator, no last-updated timestamp, no Pause feed button. ShellLayout's .page-head is also plain (shell.css lines 54-74).
gaps.notes: Build a new Topbar component (server/components/topbar.tsx) with props: title, sub, live (boolean), right slot. Add .topbar CSS to app.css or a new topbar.css. Wire it as the first child of .main (outside .main-inner). The 'live' dot requires SSE or polling state — initially can be static. 'Updated Xs ago' needs a JS counter or SSE timestamp.

gaps.design_target: Each of the 4 KPI cards contains a 84x28px SVG sparkline positioned absolute at top-right (right:12px; top:14px). Design uses a <Sparkline> component rendering area+line SVG paths with tone variants: info (--info stroke), ok (--ok stroke), warn (--warn stroke). CSS .kpi-spark { position:absolute; right:12px; top:14px; height:28px; width:80px } in design app.css.
gaps.current_state: portal KpiStrip (server/templates/fragments/kpi-strip.tsx lines 38-48) renders: label, kpi-num, and optionally kpi-sub. No sparkline slot, no absolute-positioned SVG. The .kpi card has no position:relative; overflow:hidden in portal app.css (app.css line 352: .kpi just has background/border/border-radius/padding).
gaps.notes: Add a server-rendered SVG Sparkline component. Data for the dashboard sparklines must come from the route handler (7/13/24-point time series per metric). Add position:relative and overflow:hidden to .kpi, and .kpi-spark positioning rule to app.css. The design uses client-side genWalk() random data — in the Hono port these must be real time-series from the daemon readers or at minimum a 7-point rolling window.

gaps.area: KPI strip — value size and color states
gaps.design_target: design app.css .kpi-num: font-size:28px (vs portal 26px). Burn rate tile shows .kpi-ok (color:--ok). Approvals queue shows .kpi-warn (color:--warn). kpi-sub uses flex row with gap:6px and delta-up (--ok) / delta-down (--err) spans for trend arrows. Unit span inside kpi-num: font-size:14px; color:var(--fg-2); margin-left:2px; font-weight:500.
gaps.current_state: portal app.css .kpi-num: font-size:26px. portal KpiStrip renders a plain string value with no tone variants (.kpi-ok / .kpi-warn not applied). kpi-sub is a plain <div> with no flex/gap. No delta-up/delta-down spans, no unit suffix handling. buildKpiItems() (dashboard.tsx lines 86-115) produces only {label, value, sub} — no tone, no delta, no unit.
gaps.notes: Bump .kpi-num to 28px in app.css. Extend KpiItem type to add optional tone ('ok'|'warn'|'err') and optional unit suffix. Extend KpiStrip to conditionally apply .kpi-ok/.kpi-warn class. Add delta-up/delta-down sub-spans — requires the route handler to compute period-over-period deltas. The unit suffix is cosmetic only (easy). Delta computation needs a lookback window in the readers.

gaps.area: Dashboard layout — section ordering and structure
gaps.design_target: dashboard.jsx DashboardView renders: (1) Topbar, (2) .sec > KpiStrip, (3) .sec > sec-head 'Pipeline · all in-flight' + seg control [Swimlanes|List|Timeline] + PipelineSwimlanes, (4) .grid-2 (1.4fr 1fr) > ActivityFeed section + CostMiniCard section, (5) .sec > AgentsMini. No repos section, no standards-drift, no approval-queue-strip, no active-requests table.
gaps.current_state: portal DashboardView (server/templates/views/dashboard.tsx) renders: (1) page-head, (2) KpiStrip, (3) Repos section, (4) ApprovalQueueStrip (gates), (5) StandardsDriftSummary, (6) Active requests table. Completely different section set — repos-centric rather than pipeline-swimlane-centric.
gaps.notes: This is the largest structural delta. The v3 design pivots the dashboard from a repo-grid+table model to a pipeline-swimlane+activity-feed+agents model. Full reconstruction needed: new PipelineSwimlanes component (server/templates/fragments/pipeline-swimlanes.tsx), new ActivityFeed component, AgentsMini embedded section, 14d cost bars panel, and a grid-2 two-column layout. The old regions (repos, standards-drift, approval-queue) move to their own dedicated views or become secondary panels. All dashboard route data must be restructured to provide per-phase request buckets.

gaps.design_target: design app.css: .pipeline-shell (bg-1, border, border-radius:3px, overflow:hidden). .pipeline-header: 8-col grid, bg-2, border-bottom. Each .ph-col: 10px 12px padding, mono 10px/700, uppercase, with 8x8 rounded-square phase dot (border-radius:2px) and pill count badge. .pipeline-body: 8-col grid, min-height:480px. Each .lane: border-right, padding:10px, flex-column, gap:8px. Phase dot colors set inline via window.AD.PHASE_COLOR[phase].

The dashboard has no swimlane concept. No .pipeline-shell, .pipeline-header, .pipeline-body, or .lane CSS exists in portal app.css. The portal's .pipeline class (app.css line 481) is a horizontal flex strip for single-request phase tracking.
gaps.notes: Entirely new component. The route must group requests by phase. In Hono SSR, the swimlanes are static per render (no drag-and-drop). Add .pipeline-shell, .pipeline-header, .ph-col, .ph-dot, .ph-count, .pipeline-body, .lane CSS to app.css. The 8-column grid maps to all 8 pipeline phases. Empty lanes get the dim placeholder. Cards need the left-bar color from phase tokens.

gaps.design_target: design app.css .pcard: bg-2, border, border-left:3px solid var(--lane-color), border-radius:3px, padding:10px, cursor:pointer, flex-column, gap:6px. States: .attn (warn border+shadow), .blocked (err border+shadow), .live (pulsing green dot top-right via ::after). Hover: translateY(-1px). Selected: bg-3 + 1.5px brand box-shadow. Contains: .pid (mono 10px fg-3), .ptitle (sans 12.5px/500, fg-0), .pbar (3px progress bar using --lane-color), .pfoot (agent avatar 14px circle, ETA dim, cost mono).
gaps.current_state: No .pcard component or CSS exists in the portal. The closest is app.css .repo-card which is a vertical-flex card with left border — similar shape but different content (repo name/path/meta vs request ID/title/progress/agent).
gaps.notes: New component to add. In Hono SSR the ::after live-dot animation works fine (CSS-only). The --lane-color custom property must be emitted as an inline style attribute on each .pcard. The agent avatar (.av) initials must be computed server-side from the agent name. The .pbar width must be an inline style percentage.

gaps.design_target: design ActivityFeed: .card with .card-h ('Activity feed', 'tail · last 10m', live indicator dot+Streaming). .feed rows: 4-col grid (80px timestamp | 100px chip | 1fr message | auto request-ref), font-family mono 12px. First row gets .live class: background var(--brand-tint) with @keyframes fadeBg (1.8s fade to transparent). Chip has tone variants (err/warn/ok/info) at fontSize:9px. Up to 10 rows.
gaps.current_state: No activity feed component or CSS exists anywhere in the portal. There is a log-tail component (server/templates/fragments/live-log.tsx) but it is a raw log tail in .log monospace terminal style, not the structured event feed. No .feed, .feed-row, .fadeBg animation CSS in portal.
gaps.notes: Requires a new ActivityFeed component and a new data source. The daemon must expose a recent-activity event log (last N events with timestamp, agent, action verb, request ref, tone). This is the highest data-dependency new surface. Initial implementation could use SSE via /portal/events + HTMX OOB, or polling. The fadeBg animation on the newest row requires client-side JS (re-add class on update) or server-side heuristic (mark the last event as .live on first render only).

gaps.design_target: design CostMiniCard: .card with no .card-h. .cost-bars: 14-col grid, height:110px, align-items:end, gap:4px, margin:12px 0 6px. Each .cost-bar: flex-column-reverse, gap:1px, border-radius:2px 2px 0 0, hover filter:brightness(1.12). Segments (.seg) colored inline from PHASE_COLOR[phase]. .cost-axis: 14-col grid labels (14d / 7d / now). gaps.notes: The 14-day stacked bar is a new server-rendered HTML/CSS component (no SVG needed — pure div stacking). The route handler must query 14 days of cost data broken down by phase. The phase segment heights are inline style percentages. The hover brightness filter is pure CSS. The legend reuses the existing phase color token system.

No .util-bar rule, no p50 latency data.

The chip-phase at 9px overrides the base 11px — needs a font-size inline or .chip-phase.sm variant. The util-bar brand fill is already achievable with existing token. gaps.area: Dashboard route handler — data model for swimlanes
gaps.design_target: dashboard.jsx reads window.AD.PHASES (8 phase objects with key+label), window.AD.PHASE_COLOR[phase], and requests[] with fields: id, title, phase, status ('attn'|'blocked'|live), priority (p0-p3), pct (0-100 progress), agent (name), eta, cost (float). byPhase grouping happens client-side via useMemo.
gaps.current_state: dashboard.ts computeDashboardAggregates() produces totalActive, totalGates, totalMtd, gateBreakdownText, totalBlockingHits, standardsCount, topGates[], standardsDrift[]. DashboardRequest type (types/render.ts) has: id, title, repo, phase, status('running'|'gate'|'done'), cost, gateType, waitedMin, variantLabel. Missing fields: priority, pct (progress %), agent name, eta, live boolean.
gaps.notes: Extend DashboardRequest with priority, progressPct, agentName, eta, isLive. Extend computeDashboardAggregates to return requestsByPhase: Record<phase, DashboardRequest[]>. These fields must be sourced from the intake-router state file or request-actions.json. Priority is stored in request metadata; progressPct can be derived from phase index / 8.

summary: The v3 design is a fundamentally different product vision from the current portal: it replaces a repos-grid + standards-drift + requests-table dashboard with a pipeline-swimlane + activity-feed + agents-mini dashboard. Of the 20 gaps identified, 8 are wholly new surfaces (swimlanes, pipeline cards, activity feed, 14d cost bars, agents mini-grid, inspector slide-over, topbar, sparklines) and 12 are corrections to existing CSS/tokens/layout. The largest single effort is the dashboard layout restructuring (xl) which requires new route data, 4 new components, and a new layout structure. The second-largest is the inspector slide-over (l) which requires a new shell slot and JS toggle. Four CSS-only token fixes (section-head typography, card-h sizing, seg control sizing, chip-phase token refs) are xs effort and should be landed first as they unblock visual accuracy of existing pages. The phase-color dark-theme override in static/app.css is the single highest-ROI fix: one deletion unblocks the correct phase palette across the entire portal.

purpose: Gap analysis between the v3 design prototype (request-detail.jsx) and the current Hono SSR portal implementation of the Request Detail view. Covers layout structure, phase track, artifact pane, gate panel, reviewer verdicts, diff viewer, and interactions.

gaps.area: Page-level layout: sticky topbar + two-column rdetail grid
gaps.design_target: Design uses `.main-inner` with padding: var(--pad-section) 32px; max-width: 1480px auto. The `.rdetail` grid is `grid-template-columns: minmax(0,1fr) 360px; gap: 18px`. `.rdetail-main` is a flex column with gap 18px. Phase timeline (`.phase-timeline`) is a surface card (bg-1, 1px border, radius 3px, padding 16px 18px) sitting above the rdetail grid. There is a sticky `.topbar` with blur backdrop and 12px 32px padding.
gaps.current_state: Portal uses `<main class='request-detail'>` with stacked `<section class='sec'>` fragments, no two-column grid. The `.main` in app.css is `padding: 28px 36px; max-width: 1280px` — narrower than the design's 1480px. No sticky topbar on the detail page; RequestPageHead is a simple `.page-head` div. The gate panel (GateDetail) is rendered in document flow, not a sticky right column. File: server/templates/views/request-detail.tsx, static/app.css.
gaps.notes: Requires adding `.rdetail` + `.rdetail-main` grid to app.css and refactoring RequestDetailView to wrap PipelineVis and ArtifactPane in `.rdetail-main` and GateDetail/GateActionPanel in the 360px right column. Also needs max-width bump to 1480px on .main for this page.

gaps.area: Sticky topbar on request-detail: Topbar component
gaps.design_target: Design has a `<Topbar>` at the top of the view (position sticky, top 0, z-index 10, backdrop blur 8px, border-bottom 1px line-1, padding 12px 32px). It shows request id as title, request title as sub, priority chip (brand), repo chip, author meta-mono, and a Back ghost button. Height implied ~42px.
gaps.current_state: RequestPageHead renders `<div class='page-head'>` with a `.back-row` (← Back link + r-id span) and `.head-actions` (Pause/Kill buttons). No sticky positioning, no backdrop blur, no priority/repo/author chips. File: server/templates/fragments/request-page-head.tsx, static/app.css lines 165-168.
gaps.notes: The Topbar CSS class and its sticky/blur behavior exist in design's app.css but not in the portal's app.css. Need to add `.topbar` / `.topbar-inner` CSS and update RequestPageHead to render priority, repo, author chips. Priority and author fields must be threaded from the request record to the fragment.

gaps.area: Phase pipeline: horizontal track with numbered dots vs flat pipe-step buttons
gaps.design_target: `.phase-track` is a CSS grid of 8 equal columns with a connecting hairline (::before, left 6% to right 6%, top 11px, 2px height, bg line-1). Each step is a flex column (center-aligned) with: `.dot-big` (24x24px circle, font-mono 10px 700, color fg-3; done = bg ok; now = bg brand + box-shadow 0 0 0 4px brand-tint + `pulseBrand` keyframe at 1.6s; selected = outline 2px fg-0 offset 2px), `.phase-name` (font-mono 10px 600 uppercase 0.06em; done/now = fg-0), `.phase-meta` (font-mono 10px fg-3, shows duration). Current phase (now) pulses amber via `pulseBrand` keyframe.
gaps.current_state: `.pipe-step` is a flex column (text-left) button: `flex: 1; border 1px line-1; border-right 0; padding 12px 14px`. State is conveyed by `.pipe-dot` (8x8px circle, no number) + `.pipe-name` (font-mono 11px 600) + `.pipe-state` (font-mono 10px fg-2). The `now` state colors the background brand-tint. No numbered dots, no connecting hairline, no pulsing amber animation, no per-step duration label. Files: server/templates/fragments/pipeline-vis.tsx, static/app.css lines 481-501.
gaps.notes: Structural change from flat bordered buttons to a centered dot-above-label pattern. Requires: (1) new `.phase-track` grid CSS with ::before hairline connector, (2) `.dot-big` CSS with numbered content and state variants including pulseBrand keyframe, (3) `.phase-meta` for duration display, (4) PipelineVis TSX to emit `.dot-big` with step index, duration from phase data. Also, the design supports a `blocked` state (red dot) not yet modeled in the portal.

gaps.area: Phase timeline header row: cost/budget/branch metadata inline
gaps.design_target: Inside `.phase-timeline sec`, above the track, is a flex row: `h3` (font-size 13px 600 'Phase pipeline'), a `meta-mono dim` span showing `opened {d.opened} · branch {branch}`, a spacer, and a `meta-mono dim` `cost $X.XX / $Y.YY`. This header is in the surface card wrapping the phase track.

summary: The v3 design and the current Hono portal share the same design token vocabulary (colors, typography, spacing) but diverge significantly in layout architecture and interaction model for the request-detail view. The three highest-impact gaps are: (1) the two-column rdetail grid with a 360px sticky gate panel — the portal renders everything in a single linear column; (2) the phase track interaction model — the design swaps the artifact pane inline on phase click while the portal opens a modal overlay; (3) the gate panel — the design unifies reviewer verdicts, note textarea, approve/reject, and decision banner in one sticky right-column component while the portal splits this across GateDetail and GateActionPanel in two different contexts. The phase track visual also requires a structural rewrite (numbered dots with hairline connector vs flat bordered buttons). CSS-only gaps (sec-head h2 eyebrow style, diff two-column line-number grid, density tokens) are lower effort but affect multiple pages. The topbar sticky blur header on the detail page and the cost/budget display in the pipeline header are additive changes that require minor data threading.

summary:
Design tokens and color values are in near-perfect parity between the v3 design and current portal (both share identical design-tokens.css). The main gaps fall into four tiers:

TIER 1 — Structural chrome (m effort each): (1) The sticky per-view Topbar with backdrop-blur replaces the current ShellLayout page-head; every view needs its own Topbar call. (2) The main content column needs `height:100vh; overflow-y:auto` to enable per-view scroll regions. These two changes ripple into all 8 views.

TIER 2 — View-level layout rewrites (l/xl): (3) Approvals table needs a 6-column grid with reviewer check-dots (.cdot), row selection, waiting-age column, and a gate-stats-7d card — the current GateRow is a different 3-column component. (4) Agents needs a 3-col card-grid tile layout (with util-bar, p50, MTD per agent) instead of the current flat table — data model changes required. (5) Ops needs four new tiles (Kill-tile promoted from rail, per-service breaker grid, autopilot, firewall egress) where the current view shows log-tail + deploy events. (6) Logs view needs the `.log` terminal container (not ul/li), agent-tag column, grep input, and filter strip.

TIER 3 — CSS additions (xs/s): (7) Add `.btn.ok-btn` and `.btn.xs` (used by approval row actions, currently missing). (8) Fix dark-mode phase-color overrides in app.css — they diverge from the authoritative design-tokens.css. (9) `.sec-head h2` needs mono uppercase 13px fg-2 treatment (currently 16px sans). (10) Add density token layer (`[data-density]` + `--row-y/--pad-card/--pad-section`). (11) Add `.filter-strip` + search input CSS. (12) KPI num 26→28px + sparkline slot.

TIER 4 — New data surfaces (xl): (13) Pipeline swimlane kanban board (dashboard — entirely new). (14) Costs stacked-bar chart (replaces SVG line). The inspector slide-in drawer is a polish item (new CSS + HTMX slot, m effort).

The design-tokens.css files are identical between prototype and portal (correct). The font faces, color palette, spacing scale, and animation easing all match. The gaps are structural and compositional, not token-level.

purpose: Gap analysis between the v3 design shell (shell.jsx + design app.css + design-tokens.css) and the current Hono portal shell (shell.tsx, rail-nav.tsx, brand-wordmark.tsx, shell.css, app.css, portal.css).

summary:
The v3 design and current Hono portal share the same token vocabulary (all color tokens, font families, spacing scale, radius tokens, and semantic status tokens match exactly in both light and dark themes in design-tokens.css). The nav structure is identical (9 items, two groups: Operate/System). The structural and component gaps cluster into three tiers:

TIER 1 — Structural (load-bearing for correct scroll/layout behavior):
(1) Overflow/scroll model: `.app` must use height:100vh not min-height, body needs overflow:hidden, `.main` needs its own overflow-y:auto scroll. Without this, the sticky Topbar and Inspector slide-out are broken. (2) Rail outer-padding must be removed from shell.css — children own their own padding per section. (3) A `.main-inner` wrapper with density tokens is absent; current `.main` bakes in padding directly.

TIER 2 — Missing components:
(4) Topbar is entirely absent — used on every page in the design. It is sticky, frosted (backdrop-filter:blur), and renders h1 at 17px (vs current 26-28px, the most visible visual regression). (5) Sparkline SVG primitive is missing; the `.kpi-spark` CSS slot exists but is always empty. (6) Inspector right-pane slide-out is absent; portal currently uses modal-bg overlays instead. (7) Kill-switch engaged pulse animation CSS is missing despite ShellLayout already conditionally applying the class.

TIER 3 — CSS detail fixes (quick wins, all xs/s effort):
(8) Rail-nav group-label selector in app.css (`.rail-nav .group`) does not match server-rendered class (`rail-nav-group-label`) — group labels are currently unstyled. (9) Icon slot must be inline-flex, 14px not 16px; active icon color (brand) rule is missing. (10) Count badge: non-active badge renders fg-2 text instead of bg-0/white. (11) `.rail-ops` missing `background: var(--bg-0)`. (12) Dark-theme phase colors diverge in 7 of 8 slots in app.css duplicate block. (13) Theme-toggle labels must be title-case ('Light'/'Dark') not all-caps. (14) Brand caption: margin-top 2px→4px, letter-spacing 0.06em→0.08em. (15) Density tokens missing from design-tokens.css. (16) KPI num font-size 26→28px, kpi needs overflow:hidden, kpi-sub needs delta color rules.
components: Sparkline (absent — needs new primitive in server/components/primitives.tsx)

purpose: Gap analysis between the v3 design's Tweaks Panel (tweaks-panel.jsx + TweaksPanelComp in app.jsx) and the current Hono portal's theme/settings implementation.

gaps.area: Tweaks panel component — does not exist in current portal
gaps.design_target: A floating fixed-position panel (bottom:16px right:16px, z-index:60) rendered as TweaksPanelComp in app.jsx when tweaksOpen=true. Dimensions: 280px wide, variable height. Background: var(--bg-1), border: 1px solid var(--line-2), border-radius: 4px, box-shadow: 0 12px 36px rgba(0,0,0,0.35). Header row: 'Tweaks' label (font-mono, 10px, fw700, 0.1em tracking, uppercase, fg-2) + close (x icon 12px). Contains 4 TweakSection groups: Theme, Density, Accent, Live data. Footer: hairline border-top, 10px mono fg-3 hint text 'Toggle via the Tweaks button in the toolbar.'
gaps.current_state: No floating tweaks panel exists anywhere in the Hono portal. The portal has no component, route, or JS module corresponding to TweaksPanelComp.
gaps.notes: Must be a pure-JS/CSS client-side panel since the portal is server-rendered Hono+HTMX with no React. The design's panel is React-only. Re-implement as a vanilla JS class that renders into a fixed div and is toggled by a button in rail-ops. The 'Toggle via the Tweaks button in the toolbar' footer hint also implies a trigger button needs to be added to rail-ops.

gaps.area: Theme toggle — pill in rail-ops (current) vs Segmented control inside tweaks panel (design)
gaps.design_target: Theme selection is a TweakSection titled 'Theme' inside the tweaks panel, using a Seg (segmented radio) with options [['dark','Dark'],['light','Light']]. The Seg uses CSS class .seg (display:inline-flex, bg:var(--bg-2), border:1px solid var(--line-1), border-radius:3px, padding:2px) with .active button getting background:var(--bg-1), box-shadow:0 0 0 1px var(--line-2). Design also keeps the existing theme-toggle pill in rail-ops for quick access (passed as onToggleTheme prop to RailOps).
gaps.current_state: Current portal renders a pill-style theme-toggle button at the foot of .rail-ops in shell.tsx (lines 352-363). CSS for .theme-toggle, .tt-track, .tt-knob, .tt-l defined in /static/app.css lines 111-160. Delegated click handler via data-action='toggle-theme' in theme-toggle.js. The segmented .seg class exists in app.css (line 458) but is not wired to theme selection.
gaps.notes: The design keeps the rail-ops pill AND adds the seg inside the tweaks panel. The current portal only has the pill. The pill's CSS and JS already match the design's pill spec — no change needed to theme-toggle.js or .tt-track CSS. The tweaks panel adds a second, more accessible seg-based entry point.

gaps.area: Density control — new feature, not in current portal
gaps.design_target: TweakSection titled 'Density' with a Seg offering [['comfortable','Comfortable'],['compact','Compact']]. Setting writes data-density attribute to <html>. Two CSS layers consume it: :root[data-density='comfortable'] { --row-y:12px; --pad-card:16px; --pad-section:28px } and :root[data-density='compact'] { --row-y:6px; --pad-card:12px; --pad-section:16px }. Default (no attribute) middle values: --row-y:10px; --pad-card:14px; --pad-section:22px. These tokens are defined in the design's app.css lines 14-30.
gaps.current_state: No data-density attribute, no density CSS tokens (--row-y, --pad-card, --pad-section), and no JS to write them. The current portal's app.css and design-tokens.css have no density system whatsoever. Spacing is hardcoded via --s-* tokens.
gaps.notes: Need to: (1) add the three density token blocks to app.css, (2) refactor affected components to use --row-y/--pad-card/--pad-section instead of hardcoded --s-* values where density should apply, (3) add JS in the tweaks-panel module to set data-density and persist to localStorage. Scope of refactor to use the density tokens is the main effort.

gaps.area: Accent preset swatches — new feature, not in current portal
gaps.design_target: TweakSection titled 'Accent' with 4 color swatch buttons (32x32px each, border-radius:3px). Swatches: amber, cyan, sage, rose. Active swatch: border 2px solid var(--fg-0). Inactive: 1px solid var(--line-1). Clicking a swatch calls applyTokens() which calls document.documentElement.style.setProperty() for --brand, --brand-hover, --brand-press, --brand-tint, --brand-line using per-theme preset maps. Dark presets: amber {brand:#e89255}, cyan {brand:#6fb5c9}, sage {brand:#a4c692}, rose {brand:#d99891}. Light presets: amber {brand:#c8631a}, cyan {brand:#1d7f9a}, sage {brand:#3e6f29}, rose {brand:#a8453f}.
gaps.current_state: No accent system. The portal has a single fixed --brand token per theme defined statically in design-tokens.css (light: #c8631a, dark: #e89255). No JS mechanism to override brand tokens at runtime, no accent swatch UI.
gaps.notes: The accent presets in the design perfectly match the existing dark-theme --brand (#e89255=amber) and light-theme --brand (#c8631a=amber). The portal's current amber values ARE the amber preset — so 'amber' is already the default. Only the runtime-override mechanism (JS setProperty calls) and the other 3 preset definitions (cyan, sage, rose) are new. Persist chosen accent in localStorage as 'portal-accent'.

gaps.area: Live data / streaming toggle — new feature
gaps.design_target: TweakSection titled 'Live data' with a Seg offering [['on','Streaming'],['off','Frozen']]. When 'off', the polling interval (setInterval at 2400ms in the React prototype) is cleared, freezing all dashboard card progress. In the server-rendered Hono portal, the equivalent is HTMX polling: hx-trigger='every 5s' on the main data containers. A 'Frozen' state should suspend HTMX polling by adding the htmx-frozen class or removing hx-trigger dynamically.
gaps.current_state: No live-data toggle exists. The portal uses HTMX polling (hx-trigger patterns) on various fragments. There is no mechanism to pause/resume polling. The concept of 'streaming vs frozen' does not exist in the current portal's JS or HTML.
gaps.notes: Implementation approach for Hono+HTMX: toggling 'Frozen' could remove hx-trigger attributes from polling elements or call htmx.remove() on them, while 'Streaming' restores them. Alternatively, a data-live attribute on <html> with a MutationObserver in a JS module. Complexity is moderate — HTMX does not natively support pausing individual polls without DOM manipulation.

gaps.area: Tweaks panel trigger button in rail-ops
gaps.design_target: In the design's shell.jsx, a 'Tweaks' icon button (sliders icon) is rendered in the rail-ops or topbar area that sets tweaksOpen=true. The tweaks-panel.jsx infrastructure also listens for window.__activate_edit_mode postMessage events to open the panel. The rail-ops in app.jsx receives onToggleTheme as a prop but the tweaks panel open/close is controlled from App() state.
gaps.current_state: No tweaks trigger button in shell.tsx. The rail-ops block (shell.tsx lines 316-364) has only: RailOpsRow (daemon), RailOpsRow (breaker), conditional RailOpsRow (MTD), kill-switch kbtn, and theme-toggle pill. No 'sliders' / tweaks button exists. The icons/sliders.svg exists in static/icons/ but is only referenced in rail-nav for the Settings nav item.
gaps.notes: Add a small icon button at the foot of rail-ops (or alongside the theme toggle pill) using the existing sliders.svg icon. Wire data-action='open-tweaks' with a delegated click handler in the new tweaks-panel.js module.

gaps.area: Segmented control (.seg) — functional parity for tweaks panel
gaps.design_target: The design's Seg component uses CSS class .seg (display:inline-flex, bg:var(--bg-2), border:1px solid var(--line-1), br:3px, padding:2px). Active button: .active { background:var(--bg-1); box-shadow:0 0 0 1px var(--line-2); }. Used inside the tweaks panel for Theme and Density and Live data sections.
gaps.current_state: The current portal's app.css (line 458) already defines .seg { display:inline-flex; background:var(--bg-2); border:1px solid var(--line-1); border-radius:3px; padding:2px; gap:0 } and .seg-btn.on { background:var(--bg-1); box-shadow:0 0 0 1px var(--line-2) }. The design uses class .active instead of .on for the selected state.
gaps.notes: Two options: (1) add .seg button.active as an alias of .seg-btn.on in app.css, or (2) use the existing .on class in the vanilla JS tweaks panel implementation. Option 2 avoids CSS drift. Existing .seg-btn height/font rules also need to be confirmed compatible with the tweaks panel's 11.5px / 22px height target from tweaks-panel.jsx.

gaps.design_target: The TweaksPanelComp header uses className='close-btn' for the X button. No inline style. Expected: small icon-only button, transparent background, fg-2 color, hover to fg-0. The tweaks-panel.jsx generic panel uses .twk-x (22x22px, border-radius:6px, font-size:13px, rgba(41,38,27,.55) color) but TweaksPanelComp in app.jsx uses 'close-btn' class.
gaps.current_state: No .close-btn class defined anywhere in the current portal's CSS files (app.css, shell.css, portal.css, primitives.css, design-tokens.css). The modal close button uses .modal-close (shell.css line 356).
gaps.notes: Either reuse .modal-close for the tweaks panel close button or add a .close-btn alias/rule. The icon already exists as x.svg in static/icons/.

gaps.area: Theme default — current portal defaults to 'dark'; design matches
gaps.design_target: TWEAK_DEFAULTS.theme = 'dark' in app.jsx. design-tokens.css dark mode is activated by [data-theme='dark'] on <html>. Default dark.
gaps.current_state: theme-toggle.js line 68 returns 'dark' as default. shell.tsx FOUC_PREVENTION_IIFE defaults to 'dark'. ShellLayout prop theme defaults to 'dark'. design-tokens.css dark mode is :root[data-theme='dark']. Fully matches.
gaps.notes: No change needed. Current and design are identical on dark-default behavior.

gaps.area: Theme toggle CSS — pill shape, dimensions, track labels
gaps.design_target: Design app.css (lines 198-242): .theme-toggle height:26px, width:100%, bg:var(--bg-2), border:1px solid var(--line-1), border-radius:999px, padding:2px. .tt-track: grid 1fr 1fr, font-mono 9.5px fw600 0.1em uppercase. .tt-knob: 50% width, bg:var(--bg-1), border-radius:999px, box-shadow:0 0 0 1px var(--line-2), transition translateX. .tt-track.dark .tt-knob translateX(100%). Label color states: light-active fg-0/dark-muted fg-2, dark-active fg-0/light-muted fg-2.
gaps.current_state: Current app.css lines 111-160 define identical CSS for .theme-toggle, .tt-track, .tt-knob, .tt-l. Values are pixel-for-pixel identical to design: height:26px, 999px border-radius, bg-2/bg-1 colors, font-mono 9.5px fw600 0.1em, knob transition, color state rules. The only tiny difference: current shell.css .rail-ops is styled via --s-* spacing tokens while design uses px literals — but the rendered pill itself matches exactly.
gaps.notes: Theme toggle pill CSS is already pixel-accurate. No CSS changes needed.

gaps.area: Tweaks panel panel chrome — frosted glass vs opaque (tweaks-panel.jsx vs TweaksPanelComp)
gaps.design_target: The generic tweaks-panel.jsx (the Claude Design export protocol) specifies a frosted-glass panel: background:rgba(250,249,247,.78), backdrop-filter:blur(24px) saturate(160%), border:.5px solid rgba(255,255,255,.6), border-radius:14px, box-shadow inset + drop. Font 11.5px ui-sans-serif. However, the app.jsx TweaksPanelComp overrides all this with: background:var(--bg-1), border:1px solid var(--line-2), border-radius:4px, box-shadow:0 12px 36px rgba(0,0,0,0.35). The app.jsx version is the portal-native implementation.
gaps.current_state: No tweaks panel at all. The portal's existing modal uses bg-0 background per shell.css .modal-bg>.modal.
gaps.notes: Use the TweaksPanelComp styling (var(--bg-1), border var(--line-2), border-radius:4px) NOT the generic tweaks-panel.jsx frosted glass — the app.jsx component is the portal-specific override and aligns with the portal's design language (no backdrop-filter, no 14px radius, no rgba hack).

gaps.design_target: TweakSection in app.jsx: div style font-mono, 10px, fw600, 0.08em tracking, uppercase, fg-2, margin-bottom:6px. Section container has margin-bottom:14px.
gaps.current_state: No .tweak-section CSS class exists. The portal has .h-eyebrow (design-tokens.css line 297): font-mono, t-tiny (11px), fw600, 0.08em tracking, uppercase, fg-2. Same visual intent but 11px vs 10px, and no margin-bottom:6px on the label.
gaps.notes: Add .tweak-section and .tweak-section-label rules. Can reuse .h-eyebrow as a base but override font-size to 10px. The 1px font size delta is minor but matches the design's intentionally smaller chrome text inside the compact floating panel.

gaps.area: localStorage persistence for density and accent
gaps.design_target: The design's useTweaks() in app.jsx persists all tweaks via window.parent.postMessage(__edit_mode_set_keys) — a Claude Design protocol, not localStorage. However the portal's own theme-toggle.js uses localStorage key 'portal-theme'. By analogy, density and accent need their own localStorage keys ('portal-density', 'portal-accent') with FOUC-prevention IIFEs.
gaps.current_state: theme-toggle.js persists 'portal-theme' to both localStorage and cookie (for SSR shadow via lib/theme.ts). No density or accent persistence exists.
gaps.notes: Density and accent need: (1) a FOUC-prevention IIFE in ShellLayout that reads 'portal-density' and 'portal-accent' from localStorage and sets data-density/data-accent on <html> before first paint, (2) setProperty calls in JS for accent tokens, and (3) data-density attribute switching. Cookies are optional for density/accent since there's no SSR shadow needed (unlike theme which must match the server-rendered data-theme attribute).

summary: The v3 design introduces a Tweaks Panel (TweaksPanelComp in app.jsx) that is entirely absent from the current Hono portal. This panel is a fixed-position 280px floating sheet at bottom-right that provides four runtime controls: (1) Theme toggle (dark/light segmented control), (2) Density switcher (comfortable/compact, backed by new CSS tokens --row-y/--pad-card/--pad-section), (3) four accent color presets (amber/cyan/sage/rose) that rewrite --brand* tokens at runtime via JS setProperty, and (4) a Live data streaming/frozen toggle that would pause HTMX polling. The current portal has a working theme-toggle pill in rail-ops whose CSS already exactly matches the design spec — that CSS requires zero changes. The largest new work items are: creating the vanilla-JS tweaks panel module (replacing the React implementation), adding density CSS tokens and wiring components to use them, implementing the runtime accent system, and adding a trigger button in rail-ops. Design-tokens.css is identical between the prototype and production portal, so all token references are safe to reuse. The generic tweaks-panel.jsx file is Claude Design infrastructure (frosted glass shell, postMessage protocol) that should NOT be directly ported — the portal-specific TweaksPanelComp in app.jsx with its opaque var(--bg-1) / 4px-radius aesthetic is the correct reference.

purpose: Gap analysis between the v3 design prototype (React/JSX, /tmp/design_extract/autonomous-dev-v3/) and the live Hono SSR portal (plugins/autonomous-dev-portal/). Covers every meaningful UI element: layout, tokens, components, interactions, and states. Used to plan the port effort.
summary: The token foundation (colors, type scale, radii, shadows) is essentially identical between design and portal — both files were generated from the same design-system bundle. The structural shell (220px rail, 2-col grid, brand wordmark, RailNav groups, RailOps 3-line metrics, theme toggle) is fully implemented in the Hono portal. The large gaps are entirely NEW surface area the v3 design introduces that does not exist anywhere in the portal: (1) a sticky frosted-glass `.topbar` with live-indicator and page sub-title that replaces the static `.page-head`; (2) a `.main-inner` content wrapper with `max-width: 1480px` and density-token-driven padding; (3) a density-token system (`data-density` + `--row-y`/`--pad-card`/`--pad-section`); (4) complete Dashboard redesign — pipeline swimlanes (`.pipeline-shell`/`.pcard`), live activity feed (`.feed-row`), cost stacked-bar mini-chart (`.cost-bars`/`.cost-axis`), sparkline KPI tiles (`.kpi-spark`), agents mini-grid, plus a 14d cost widget; (5) richer Approvals surface with inline table header, `.approval-row` grid, `.cdot` check-squares, filter strip, and gate-stats sidebar; (6) full Request Detail rework — `.phase-timeline`/`.phase-track`/`.dot-big` 8-step stepper and `.rdetail` two-col layout with inspector pane; (7) Ops page with `.ops-tile`/`.kill-tile`/`.breaker-grid`; (8) `.inspector` slide-in overlay; and (9) a full kill-switch double-confirm modal with typed-confirmation input. Several token-level deltas also exist: `.kpi-num` is 28px in design vs 26px in portal; `.kpi-strip` gap is 12px vs 14px; `position:relative; overflow:hidden` is missing from `.kpi` tiles; `.sec-head h2` is `font-size:13px; uppercase; mono; color:var(--fg-2)` in design vs `16px; sans; no-uppercase` in portal; `.rail-brand .wm` is 15px in design vs 16px in portal; `.rail-ops` uses `background:var(--bg-0)` in design vs implicit in portal; `.rail-nav-group-label` uses `--fg-3` in design vs `--fg-2` in portal; `.kbtn.engaged` pulsing animation and `.pulse-err` keyframes are missing in the portal's app.css (but the kbtn exists); and the `[data-theme="light"] .rail-nav-item .count` override is present only in design. Phase colors in the portal's app.css dark-mode override are slightly lighter aliases than the design's design-tokens.css dark set — the portal's vendored app.css uses its own set rather than the canonical design-tokens.css dark values.

summary:
The v3 design extract introduces 3 tiers of change relative to the portal's current CSS:

CRITICAL TOKEN DRIFT: The portal's app.css (lines 46-53) contains a stale dark-theme block that re-declares all 8 phase colors with OLD values, overriding the correct v3 values already present in design-tokens.css. This is the single highest-impact bug — every phase chip, lane, and card border renders the wrong color in dark mode. The correct v3 dark phase values (d6c4ee/3d6f94/a8e0c4/6f6c20/f5b888/955d35/c4e8c6/706e5c) are in design-tokens.css but the stale app.css block (a48bd9/6fa8c7/66b896/b5b250/e89255/c98a55/98c39a/9a978a) overrides them.

NEW COMPONENT SYSTEMS: v3 introduces 7 entirely new component blocks absent from the portal: (1) density tokens ([data-density] system with --row-y/--pad-card/--pad-section), (2) pipeline swimlane kanban board (.pipeline-shell, .pipeline-header, .lane, .pcard with ~145 lines), (3) phase timeline (.phase-timeline, .phase-track, .dot-big with pulseBrand animation), (4) right-side inspector panel (.inspector slide-over, 480px), (5) redesigned approval-row with 6-column grid and .cdot check visualization, (6) stacked cost bar chart (.cost-bars/.cost-bar), and (7) sparkline component (.spark).

STYLE VALUE DRIFTS (existing components): .sec-head h2 changes from 16px sans-serif to 13px mono uppercase (biggest visible change after phase colors); .kpi-num grows from 26px to 28px; .card-h padding tightens from 14px to 12px; .card-h h3 shrinks from 14px to 13px; .filter-strip/.seg active class renamed from .on to .active and height from 26px to 28px; .topbar replaces .page-head for the main content area title with sticky blur effect; .log class names change (l-time→t, l-info→info, l-mark→brand); .diff gains second line-number column and .hunk rows; .gate-panel gains reviewer verdict rows and note textarea; max-width shifts from 1280px to 1480px; .rdetail replaces .split with wider 360px right column.

Target files by change count: app.css (28 gaps), primitives.css (4 gaps), design-tokens.css (3 gaps), shell.css (4 gaps), portal.css (3 gaps).
